title: Harbor South — Façade Color Studio
subtitle: 1766 Cape Coral Pkwy E · 6 Floors · 3 Stair Towers · Color Refresh Workspace

footer.left: Harbor South Condominium Association · Color Study Tool
footer.right: Reference: City of Cape Coral LDC Article 5 §5.8.9 · Article 4 §4.2.15

photosCaption: Existing scheme: cream body, brick-red accent panels, charcoal stair towers, white trim. Live recoloring above shows proposed scheme applied to architect's A-04 rendering.

compliance.title: Cape Coral §5.8.9 Compliance
compliance.regulationText: LDC §5.8.9 requires neutral / earth-tone façade colors with bright accent colors limited to ≤15% of the façade.

palettes.name: Existing — Cream & Brick Red
palettes.desc: Current scheme. Brick red accents, beige body, charcoal towers.

palettes.name: Option B — A-04 Coastal Teal (proposed)
palettes.desc: Architect's proposed direction. Soft teal towers and accents on white body.

palettes.name: Modern Coastal — White & Bronze
palettes.desc: Contemporary SW Florida. Crisp white with warm bronze accents.

palettes.name: Bimini Square — Warm Sand & Sage
palettes.desc: Aligned with new Bimini Basin developments down the road.

palettes.name: Gulf Mist — Soft Greige & Stone Blue
palettes.desc: Quiet, neutral-forward. Reads timeless under harsh Cape Coral sun.

palettes.name: Key West — Cream & Coral
palettes.desc: Pastel coastal vernacular. Approved range per FL design overlays.

palettes.name: Calusa Earth — Limestone & Clay
palettes.desc: Indigenous-inspired earth tones. Highest neutrality compliance score.

palettes.name: Riverfront Modern — White & Slate
palettes.desc: Minimal, high-contrast. Pairs with bronze impact window frames.

palettes.name: Sunset Stucco — Beige & Terracotta
palettes.desc: Classic Mediterranean / Florida. Terracotta on accent panels only.

palettes.name: Sea Oats — Driftwood & Pale Gold
palettes.desc: Reads natural and quiet from the bridge. Low-maintenance visual.
